public_reputation: Known around Panim Haven through day-to-day work as scribe.
manner: Listens for contradictions, omissions, and names that do not quite align, then files them where they can matter later. In Panim Haven, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of scribe in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of scribe while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of scribe credible while absorbing the strain created by divine balance obligations.

adventure_hooks.summary: Caldor Sepulcher can draw the party into a locality problem centered on scribe duties in Panim Haven.
Caldor Sepulcher becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with divine balance obligations around Panim Haven.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Caldor Sepulcher is immediately readable as a scribe tied to Panim Haven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Caldor Sepulcher presents as someone shaped by the duties of a scribe and the daily pressures of Panim Haven.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a scribe, and proof that strangers will not make Panim Haven harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Caldor Sepulcher refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a scribe too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can notice inconsistencies, formalize statements, and preserve proof that others hoped would blur.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Caldor Sepulcher adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Caldor Sepulcher always knows more about Panim Haven than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a scribe in Panim Haven.
